content: We can reframe biological and chemical pathogenic agents from the traditional definition of "agent" through the lens of the 2025 ICM curiosity mechanism, framing pathogen mutations as an intrinsic curiosity-driven reinforcement learning process where pathogens gain internal "rewards" for discovering novel unexploited host niches. This perspective suggests that emerging zoonotic spillover events are not random accidents, but predictable outcomes of a pathogen agent's intrinsic motivation to explore novelty.
